definition: The thin semantic core of the Web Charts standard: a chart declared as data → mark → encoding (Vega-Lite's vocabulary, borrowed) restricted to a minimal L1 subset — basic marks, position/color/size channels, and scales/axes/legends. Selections, transforms, and view composition are additive L2+ tiers a conformant L1 renderer may ignore. It describes what the data means, never how it is drawn.
usage: Standardized by Web Charts (#105, scaffolded under #570). The semantic plane is kept strictly separate from the presentation/theme plane (which consumes Web Theme tokens), so one spec re-themes without edits. Authored as a profile schema on the webcharts project page (#572).
